mechanism: Anthropomorphized shadow-as-bad-hider: a shadow is imagined trying to hide, but its literal physical nature is to follow a person. The comic failure is visual, universal, clean, and does not depend on homophones, idioms, or culture-specific knowledge.

en.setup: Why is a shadow so bad at hiding?
en.combined: Why is a shadow so bad at hiding? Because it always follows someone.
en.portability_notes: Uses the universal behavior of shadows following people; no English-specific wordplay.

fr.setup: Pourquoi une ombre se cache-t-elle si mal ?
fr.combined: Pourquoi une ombre se cache-t-elle si mal ? Parce qu’elle suit toujours quelqu’un.
fr.portability_notes: Natural French phrasing keeps the same bad-at-hiding setup and follows-someone reveal.

es.setup: ¿Por qué una sombra se esconde tan mal?
es.combined: ¿Por qué una sombra se esconde tan mal? Porque siempre sigue a alguien.
es.portability_notes: Avoids regional hide-and-seek vocabulary; the hiding/following mechanism remains direct.

ja.setup: どうして影は隠れるのが下手なの？
ja.punchline: いつも誰かについていくから。
ja.combined: どうして影は隠れるのが下手なの？ いつも誰かについていくから。
ja.portability_notes: Uses common Japanese words and script; the shadow-following image is immediate and unchanged.
